title: FOMC FAQs: Summary of Economic Projections
summary: This Federal Reserve FAQ page explains the mechanics of how the FOMC collects and publishes economic projections from policymakers. Projections are collected four times yearly (March, June, September, December) and cover real GDP growth, unemployment rate, PCE inflation, and core PCE inflation. The page defines how ranges and central tendencies are calculated but does not provide actual projection figures.
key_points: Economic projections collected from Board of Governors members and Federal Reserve Bank presidents four times per year
Projections include real GDP growth, unemployment rate, PCE inflation, and core PCE inflation measured Q4-to-Q4
March/June projections cover current year, next two years, and longer run; September/December extend to three years forward
Longer-run projections represent expected convergence rates absent further shocks under appropriate monetary policy
Central tendency excludes three highest and three lowest projections from the range
Initial projections due Friday before FOMC meeting; revisions allowed until morning of second meeting day
why_it_matters: Understanding FOMC projection methodology is foundational for interpreting Fed communications, but this FAQ contains no specific forecasts, data points, or current macro catalysts. It is an educational page about Fed process, not a source of market-relevant information.
